CheckTitle: AI agent policies must not contain wildcard actions or resource ARNs
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Checks both policy action statements and resource ARN statements for wildcard entries. Wildcard actions (`s3:*`, `*:*`) allow every operation in a service or across all services. Wildcard resource ARNs (`*`) allow the action on every resource in the account. Both must be replaced with explicit enumerations.
Risk: Wildcard actions combined with wildcard resources grant unrestricted access equivalent to an administrator policy. A single agent compromise can result in mass data exfiltration, resource destruction, or lateral movement to any service in the account.

Code.Other:
1. Use IAM Access Analyzer policy generation to derive a minimal policy from CloudTrail activity.
2. Replace `Action: '*'` and `Action: '<service>:*'` with the specific actions observed in CloudTrail.
3. Replace `Resource: '*'` with the ARNs of specific resources the agent needs to access.
4. Validate with `aws iam simulate-principal-policy` that all legitimate operations still work.
5. Deploy the scoped policy and monitor CloudTrail for any access-denied errors.
6. Re-run this check to confirm the wildcard entries are gone.

Recommendation.Text: Enumerate all actions and resource ARNs required by the agent and replace wildcards with explicit lists. Use IAM Access Analyzer policy generation to produce a policy based on observed CloudTrail activity. Enforce a permission boundary that blocks any wildcard-action grants from taking effect.

Notes: ASPM-016 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
